that accelerates the discovery of new altermagnetic materials with targeted
physical properties. The input consists of crystal structure data (represented
as graphs) from databases such as the Materials Project, including a large
unlabeled set for pre-training and a small labeled set of known altermagnets
(148 positive samples) for fine-tuning. The output is a list of candidate
materials predicted to be altermagnets with high probability, along with their
electronic structure properties confirmed by first-principles calculations

contains a large number of unlabeled crystal structure graphs (5,000 samples)

capturing general features that aid downstream tasks.

• The best-performing tested method was the weighted-loss supervised GNN.
• On the evaluated split, it achieved the strongest validation PR-AUC and the
best candidate retrieval at top-50 among the tested methods.
• The tested self-supervised pretraining improved some ranking calibration
metrics but did not beat weighted BCE on top-k discovery.
• A full 3-seed run for every ablation was computationally heavy in the
CPU-only sandbox, so the final ablation comparison in the report is clearly
labeled as a controlled pilot comparison on one frozen split, while the full
baseline pipeline was implemented and partially executed.
